description:
A Data Analyst at a utility company is evaluating a Virtual Power Plant (VPP) program consisting of five residential PV systems in Phoenix, Arizona. Using PySAM (Pvwattsv8) and the Phoenix, AZ TMY weather file (path in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt), simulate the aggregate performance of these systems.

Each of the five systems has a 5.0 kW DC capacity, 22.0-degree tilt, and array type 1 (Fixed roof mount), with azimuths of 90, 135, 180, 225, and 270. All other parameters should use PVWatts defaults.

Save the Python script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/vpp_simulation.py and the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/vpp_fleet_results.json. The JSON output must follow this structure:
{
    "total_fleet_capacity_kw": 25.0,
    "total_annual_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "peak_hourly_generation_kw": <float>,
    "peak_generation_hour_index": <integer, 0 to 8759>,
    "individual_annual_energies": {
        "90": <float>,
        "135": <float>,
        "180": <float>,
        "225": <float>,
        "270": <float>
    }
}
